subject: Birgma Risk Register
description: Controls-as-code self-assessment: the application's OWN security and governance controls, mapped to framework clauses with evidence pointers into the repository. Validated in CI by compliance/validate.mjs.

scopedOut.reason: The system contains no AI/ML component; an AI management system is not applicable.
No AI system is placed on the market or put into service; obligations do not apply.

controls.title: Authentication via Microsoft Entra ID (OIDC + PKCE)
controls.statement: Users authenticate with Entra ID using the Authorization-Code flow with PKCE; the API validates RS256 access tokens against JWKS, pinned to issuer, audience and tenant.

controls.title: Role-based access control (RBAC)
controls.statement: Every mutating route is gated by a role check derived from the token's role claims; reads require any recognized role.

controls.title: Object-level authorization (ownership)
controls.statement: Beyond RBAC, writes to a risk require ownership/stakeholder membership unless the actor holds an elevated role; enforced server-side and integration-tested (no IDOR).

controls.title: Input validation and output encoding
controls.statement: All request bodies and query parameters are parsed and validated with Zod schemas at the interface boundary; invalid input is rejected with 400.

controls.title: Encryption in transit (TLS)
controls.statement: The NGINX edge terminates TLS for all client traffic; the database connection supports verified TLS via DATABASE_SSL.

controls.title: Append-only audit trail
controls.statement: Every business mutation writes an immutable audit_event in the same transaction; UPDATE/DELETE are revoked from the application role and blocked by a DB trigger.

controls.title: Least-privilege database role
controls.statement: The application connects as a NOLOGIN-derived rr_api role with only the grants it needs; migrations/seed run as the owner.

controls.title: Segregation of duties (maker-checker approvals)
controls.statement: Proposed risk changes are recorded as change requests and take effect only when a DIFFERENT elevated user approves; the maker cannot approve their own change and non-elevated roles cannot decide.

controls.title: Optimistic concurrency (data integrity)
controls.statement: Risk edits carry a version; an If-Match check-and-set rejects stale writes with 409 instead of silently overwriting concurrent changes.

controls.title: Security headers, CORS and rate limiting
controls.statement: Helmet sets hardening headers; CORS is a single-origin allowlist; a per-window rate limiter caps request volume; JSON bodies are capped at 256 kb.

controls.title: Structured logging with correlation and redaction
controls.statement: pino-http emits structured logs, honours/mints an X-Request-Id for correlation, and redacts authorization and cookie headers.

controls.title: Monitoring, tracing and health checks
controls.statement: API and worker expose Prometheus /metrics (RED + runtime + queue depth) and DB-checked readiness/liveness endpoints; opt-in OpenTelemetry distributed tracing (OTLP) auto-instruments HTTP/Express/pg, correlated with the request-id logs.

controls.title: Secrets management
controls.statement: No secrets are committed; runtime configuration is injected from the environment / a vault, documented via .env.example and ADR-0015, and CI fails on committed secrets.

controls.title: Supply-chain scanning and SBOM
controls.statement: Reproducible npm ci installs; Dependabot updates; npm audit and Trivy gates on production advisories; a CycloneDX SBOM is produced in CI.

controls.title: Static application security testing
controls.statement: CodeQL analyses the TypeScript/JavaScript for semantic vulnerability patterns on every PR and weekly; Trivy scans IaC/config for misconfiguration.

controls.title: Hardened container runtime
controls.statement: Services ship as distroless, non-root images; k8s runs them read-only-rootfs with all capabilities dropped and seccomp RuntimeDefault.

controls.title: Network segmentation
controls.statement: A default-deny NetworkPolicy restricts pod-to-pod traffic; only the API and worker may reach the database.

controls.title: Backup and disaster recovery
controls.statement: Nightly logical backups (pg_dump CronJob) plus scripted backup/restore and a rehearsed DR runbook with RPO/RTO targets; managed PITR is the target for finer recovery.

controls.title: Data minimization
controls.statement: Only the object id, display name and email are provisioned from token claims; no broader directory data is stored.

controls.title: Subject-rights and retention tooling
controls.statement: DSAR export, append-only-preserving erasure, and notification retention via the privacy CLI; supported by ROPA, DPIA, privacy notice, breach runbook and a retention schedule.

controls.title: Encryption at rest
controls.statement: Host/volume disk encryption and customer-managed keys for the database and object storage.

controls.title: Multi-factor authentication / Conditional Access
controls.statement: MFA and Conditional Access are enforced at the identity provider (Entra) for all users; the API trusts Entra's authentication decision and authorizes from token claims.
